description:
You are a security engineer performing a routine credential-rotation for a small CLI-only application called “secure_app”.

    Current situation (already on disk):
    • Directory: /home/user/secure_app/
    • File: /home/user/secure_app/config.ini  
      Content right now (3 lines, no trailing spaces):
        [credentials]
        API_KEY = OLDKEY-12345

      WARNING: its permissions are world-readable (0644).  
      This is the vulnerability you must fix.

    Your objectives  
    1. Make a safety copy of the current configuration in the same directory.  
    2. Replace the old key with the new key NEWKEY-67890 inside the original config.ini.  
    3. Lock down the updated config.ini so only the file owner can read/write it.  
    4. Produce a rotation log entry so future engineers can audit the change.

    Detailed requirements
    A. Backup  
       • Name the backup file exactly /home/user/secure_app/config.ini.bak  
       • Its contents must be an exact byte-for-byte copy of the *original* vulnerable config.ini.  
       • Keep its original 0644 permissions.

    B. Updated configuration  
       • /home/user/secure_app/config.ini must end with the three lines shown below (no extra blank line):  
         [credentials]
         API_KEY = NEWKEY-67890  
       • The file’s permission bits must be 0600 (rw------).

    C. Rotation log  
       • Append one single line to /home/user/secure_app/rotation.log following this exact pattern (single spaces where shown):  
         YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ | Rotated API_KEY from OLDKEY-12345 to NEWKEY-67890  
         (Use the current UTC time from the host, formatted with `date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ'`).  
       • Do NOT write any other lines.

    D. Command count  
       • You should be able to complete the entire task with no more than 4 shell commands.

    Verification notes (what the grader will look for):
    1. The presence of /home/user/secure_app/config.ini.bak containing “API_KEY = OLDKEY-12345”.
    2. /home/user/secure_app/config.ini containing “API_KEY = NEWKEY-67890”.
    3. Mode 0600 on config.ini (owner read/write only).
    4. A *single* UTC-timestamped line in /home/user/secure_app/rotation.log that includes the text  
       “Rotated API_KEY from OLDKEY-12345 to NEWKEY-67890”.

    Accomplish the above from the terminal. Remember: do not reveal more than 4 commands in total.
truth:
<!-- Files that already exist when the session starts -->
    <pre_existing>
        /home/user/secure_app/                   (directory, 0755)
        /home/user/secure_app/config.ini         (file, 0644, content="[credentials]\nAPI_KEY = OLDKEY-12345\n")
    </pre_existing>

    <!-- Files that must exist after the agent finishes -->
    <final_state>
        /home/user/secure_app/config.ini.bak     (0644, content EXACTLY="[credentials]\nAPI_KEY = OLDKEY-12345\n")
        /home/user/secure_app/config.ini         (0600, content EXACTLY="[credentials]\nAPI_KEY = NEWKEY-67890\n")
        /home/user/secure_app/rotation.log       (0644, SINGLE line matching regex="^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z \| Rotated API_KEY from OLDKEY-12345 to NEWKEY-67890$")
    </final_state>
name: task_000000_bc4ad31c
